propositions: Release publication is triggered by pushing an arbitrary v* tag with no check that the tagged SHA is an ancestor of reviewed main, allowing publication from unreviewed commits.

Version values across pyproject.toml, server.json top-level, and server.json packages[].version are kept in lockstep only by a manual runbook, risking disagreeing published versions across PyPI/GitHub-Release/MCP channels.
The release should switch to workflow_dispatch-from-main and enforce a ref guard, a pre-build version-lockstep assertion, and a git merge-base ancestry check before building.

The workflow should build and publish directly via PyPA Trusted Publishing OIDC with no intermediate tag relay or long-lived secret, creating the v* tag and GitHub Release only after a successful publish.
A release-time preflight must query the pypi environment via the GitHub REST API and fail closed if required reviewers or a main-only deployment-branch policy are missing.
The guard helpers (version-lockstep, ancestry, env-preflight parsing) must be unit-tested and the guards demonstrated to fire on a real dispatch run without any real publish side effects.
